mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, mechanically depressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive returns exceed 55% when rates rise.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate breaches 4.5%, it signals labor market deterioration and impending recessionary pressures, causing investors to price in lower corporate profits and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5% for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, which directly boosts corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative forward returns in greater than 50% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance with p greater than 0.10 in out-of-sample regression.

mechanism_narrative: In a high inflation environment, rising CPIAUCSL forces the Federal Reserve to pause or reverse tightening, triggering a relief rally in equities that generates positive S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in less than 45% of high-inflation trigger events, or the signal reverses direction during disinflationary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in the index of industrial production reflect expanding manufacturing and utility output, which correlates with higher corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.01 over any 24-month rolling window, or forward returns are uncorrelated with INDPRO changes.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly nonfarm payrolls increase exceeding 200,000 signals robust labor demand and consumer spending capacity, which reinforces earnings growth expectations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases > 200k in a single month
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in greater than 55% of triggered months, or the threshold effect disappears when controlling for GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth decelerates while inflation remains elevated, the resulting stagflationary dynamic compresses profit margins and forces monetary tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict negative returns with accuracy below 40%, or the interaction term is statistically insignificant in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: During early-cycle recovery phases, rising industrial production indicates capacity utilization improvements and margin expansion, which directly translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal yields negative returns in greater than 60% of early-cycle observations, or the predictive power vanishes when GDP growth exceeds 3.0% annually.
